name: rail-nest-common
description: Shared NestJS infrastructure for the rail-projects workspace: ApiKeyGuard, @Public, Neo4jExceptionFilter, ValidationPipe + Throttler factories.
author: Craig Nevins (Nev433)

scripts.prepare: npm run bootstrap-deps && npm run build && npm run cleanup:runtime-deps
scripts._comment_bootstrap: npm runs prepare when a consumer installs this package via `file:` ref, BEFORE running `npm install` inside the linked directory. That means devDeps (including tsc) are not yet present and `npm run build` would fail with `sh: tsc: command not found`. The first step here populates node_modules with --ignore-scripts so we don't recurse into our own prepare. Re-runs are idempotent — `npm install` with an already-populated node_modules is a no-op.
scripts._comment_cleanup: After install + build, remove peer-dep runtimes from this package's own node_modules so consumers' copies are the ones loaded at runtime. Without this, Node's symlink-resolution walks up into THIS package's node_modules first and finds duplicate @nestjs/* classes, breaking Nest DI (Reflector class identity mismatch).
scripts.cleanup:runtime-deps: rm -rf node_modules/@nestjs node_modules/express node_modules/rxjs node_modules/reflect-metadata node_modules/neo4j-driver
